title: Brazil's booming crude exports
summary:
Main
"A shift to Europe and rising VLCC demand"
Since 2016, Brazilian crude oil exports have grown by nearly 1 million b/d, averaging 1.6 million b/d in 2024 so far. Most of Brazil's crude exports are destined for Asia, with China alone accounting for 40%. However, since 2022, an increasing share has been directed to Europe, compensating for the crude supplies Europe lost from Russia.
